rag_rules: Place AGREE annex blocks in the target component type or implementation scope as appropriate
Preserve existing AADL architecture by only adding annex blocks, not modifying existing elements
Use visible identifiers when available, but do not assume they exist without explicit confirmation
Do not use subcomponent paths in component type annex blocks
Only insert annex blocks that have been validated by the validator
generic_rules: Maintain architectural integrity by only modifying the target component or implementation
Preserve existing connections and subcomponents when inserting annex blocks
Do not introduce new dependencies or assumptions without explicit validation
Use the most specific scope possible for each annex block
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts
Do not assume AADL properties are automatically available as AGREE variables
Do not modify existing AADL elements when inserting annex blocks
irrelevant_topics: AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees
